message:
fix(core,cli): harden evidence collection and expand cross-crate integration tests

Update beads issue tracker, enhance ffs-cli evidence command, improve
ffs-core library internals, and add new cross-crate integration test
coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Align the call sites with the current VFS/OpenFs signatures and restore cargo check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:16:35.069932008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:20.424758346Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:20.424129254Z","close_reason":"Superseded: current workspace passes rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets; stale local error log only","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Missing: xattr ops, symlinks, hard links, statfs, setattr/chmod, large file writes, fsync. Mirror ext4 test structure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:21:16.191291287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T18:56:59.617154163Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T18:56:59.617125539Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in current tree; validated with rch test listing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-b182","title":"Add ext4 rename test: directory over non-empty directory returns ENOTEMPTY","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:57.126732789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:22:18.469222777Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:22:18.469014021Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 rename test: directory over non-empty directory returns ENOTEMPTY. Verifies source dir, dest dir, and dest children all survive the failed rename. Passes alongside existing btrfs equivalent.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-b5on","title":"Implement ext4 indirect block addressing and ZERO_RANGE fixes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:02.268634162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:16.655433191Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:48:16.654585948Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 indirect block addressing:
(1) resolve_indirect_block(): resolves logical blocks via 12 direct + 1 indirect + 1 double-indirect + 1 triple-indirect pointers in i_block[0..14]
(2) read_ext4_indirect(): reads file data block-by-block using indirect resolver
(3) Wired into FsOps::read() ext4 path: checks EXT4_EXTENTS_FL; if not set, uses indirect addressing
(4) Removed EXTENTS from REQUIRED_V1 incompat set — both extents and indirect now supported
Also fixed 3 bugs in review: compressed extent size overflow, decompressed size validation, inline data xattr parsing.
473 ffs-core tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","indirect","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-b80","title":"Repair: Implement scrub pipeline (detect corruption signals)","description":"Goal: detect corrupted blocks/metadata reliably.

Deliverables:
- Define signals:
  - ext4 metadata_csum / btrfs csum verification failures
  - structural invariant violations (bounds, tree ordering)
- Implement ScrubReport with per-block findings and severity.

Acceptance:
- Harness can inject corruption (flip bit) and scrub reports it deterministically.
- Scrub does not panic on corrupted data; it returns structured findings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:28.702412790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.268015147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:16.267938143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-25k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.916224379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b80","depends_on_id":"bd-2vt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:00.995317684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-bbmh","title":"Docs: align shared fixture references with actual conformance fixture tree","description":"AGENTS.md currently says shared cross-component fixtures live under tests/fixtures, but the live conformance harness, CLI usage text, and integration tests use conformance/fixtures. Update the project instructions so the documented fixture paths match the real repo layout and harness commands. Acceptance: AGENTS.md fixture references point to conformance/fixtures where appropriate, with tests/fuzz corpus references left intact.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:35:34.200068745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:37:30.863053286Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:37:30.862783315Z","close_reason":"Aligned AGENTS.md fixture-path references with the actual split between conformance/fixtures and tests/fixtures","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","harness"]}
 {"id":"bd-bejk","title":"Fix dx_root test layout, read-directory EISDIR check, and close stale clippy bead","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:41.502809158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.140304578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:23:54.139616074Z","close_reason":"Fixed 3 issues:
(1) ffs-core read() missing EISDIR check: reading a directory inode via FsOps::read() returned Ok with dir data instead of Err(EISDIR). Added is_dir() guard in ext4 read path. 471 ffs-core tests pass.
(2) ffs-ondisk parse_dx_root_basic test: dx_entry layout was wrong — test wrote explicit hash(4)+block(4) for entry 0, but dx_countlimit overlaps entry 0 (hash is implicit 0, only block at offset+4). Fixed test data layout.
(3) ffs-ondisk proptest dx_entries: same layout bug — restructured to match actual format with entry 0 block separate from subsequent hash+block pairs. 360 ffs-ondisk tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","correctness","ondisk"]}

 {"id":"bd-c35","title":"Track: Harness + Conformance + Parity Accounting (ffs-harness)","description":"Build the proof system: fixture generation, comparisons against Linux behavior where applicable, parity reporting, and regression gates.\n\nAcceptance: every implemented behavior has (1) a fixture or property test, (2) a harness test that exercises it end-to-end, (3) FEATURE_PARITY + harness parity numbers updated in the same change. Benchmarks are added only after fixtures exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:52.148014837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385993623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385971391Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-tasks closed: ext4 fixtures (bd-2zg), parity accounting (bd-6st), fixture workflow (bd-3q4), kernel reference capture (bd-2ij), btrfs fixtures (bd-2yi).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.179975971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.099617897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.014650837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.923718680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.825671893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-c35","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ffs-harness is the proof engine.

Rules:
- every new behavior needs fixtures or properties
- parity numbers must match FEATURE_PARITY.md
- only optimize after correctness vectors exist

Without this track, progress becomes vibes instead of verified parity.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-g30","title":"Track: CLI + TUI (ffs-cli + ffs-tui)","description":"Provide operator tooling: CLI subcommands (inspect/info/fsck/mount), and a frankentui-based dashboard to observe cache/MVCC/repair stats.\n\nAcceptance: CLI is the primary UX for harness + debugging; TUI can attach to a running mount to show internal metrics. Both must be deterministic/testable and use ffs-core as the integration surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:24.040606496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:14.103200953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:14.103175686Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: clap CLI, mount, fsck/scrub, TUI dashboard","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-23n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.362716892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-23r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.620739199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-2fs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.448460236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-3sk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:12.268311230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-3sp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:12.183821788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-3vn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.275857697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-m35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.535816523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-g30","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Operator UX track.

CLI is the debugging and automation surface:
- stable JSON output options

- parity/stats commands

TUI is phased and depends on metrics surfaces from cache/MVCC/repair.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-g59h","title":"Honor BackpressureDecision::Throttle in FUSE write path","description":"FrankenFuse::should_shed currently only checks for BackpressureDecision::Shed, so Degraded/Critical throttle decisions are effectively ignored and writes proceed at full speed. Apply throttle behavior (delay) for Throttle decisions and add regression test coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:51:33.860444149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:28.420379182Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:28.420348394Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","correctness","fuse"],"comments":[{"id":288,"issue_id":"bd-g59h","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented BackpressureDecision::Throttle handling in FrankenFuse::should_shed: degraded/critical throttle now sleeps for BACKPRESSURE_THROTTLE_DELAY and proceeds instead of being ignored. Added regression test should_shed_with_degraded_gate_throttles_without_shedding. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p ffs-fuse should_shed_with_degraded_gate_throttles_without_shedding; cargo test -p ffs-fuse should_shed_with_emergency_gate_sheds_writes; cargo test -p ffs-fuse should_shed_with_normal_gate_proceeds_all; cargo check/clippy -p ffs-fuse --all-targets; cargo test -p ffs-fuse.","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:52:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-g6sl","title":"Validate unignored lightweight fuse_e2e tests and stabilize failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:18:19.958578867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:21:13.510646542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:21:13.510624129Z","close_reason":"Validated with rch: fuse_e2e lightweight unignored tests pass (10/0)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -538,7 +540,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","title":"Implement verification runner conventions and machine-parseable result format","description":"## Objective
Implement shared runner conventions for deterministic E2E execution and machine-parseable result publication.

## Scope
1. Standard command envelope and environment capture for E2E invocations.
2. Stable pass/fail JSON result schema with scenario-level details.
3. Exit-code and retry semantics for CI/nightly modes.
4. Conformance checks ensuring per-epic scripts adhere to runner contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runner conventions are adopted by all verification gates.
- Machine-parseable result format is validated in automated tests.
- Inconsistent script behavior is detected and reported with actionable output.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:51:44.682751429Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T13:51:44.682038293Z","close_reason":"Implemented verification runner conventions: (1) verification_runner.rs in ffs-harness with parse_e2e_output(), build_manifest_from_parsed(), check_script_conformance(), aggregate_verdict(), merge_scenarios() — 21 unit tests. (2) Extended lib.sh with e2e_emit_json_summary() (auto-emits result.json on exit), e2e_retry() for CI mode. (3) Created run_gate.sh runner wrapper with --ci/--retries/--catalog/--conformance flags, gate-level JSON manifest output. (4) E2E script ffs_verification_runner_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios. Clippy clean, 199 harness tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.645464252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.885423555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.079471552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","title":"Verification Gate: cross-epic verification toolchain contract (unit + e2e + logging audit)","description":"## Objective
Final acceptance gate for the shared verification contract used across all hardening epics.

## Required Validation
1. Unit tests for scenario-ID, log-schema, and artifact-manifest validators.
2. End-to-end dry runs across representative epics proving runner/result-schema compliance.
3. Structured-log audit proving required correlation fields are present and validated.

## Gate Commands
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness -- --nocapture
- cross-epic E2E dry-run pack with manifest/result-schema validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared verification contract is executable and enforced.
- All epic verification gates are wired to this contract gate.
- Contract drift is automatically detected before release acceptance.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214964113Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214268861Z","close_reason":"Final acceptance gate for verification contract: (1) Extended verification_runner.rs with validate_scenario_ids(), validate_gate_contract(), structured logging with operation_id/scenario_id/outcome correlation fields — 26 unit tests. (2) Created ffs_verification_gate_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios covering: artifact_manifest tests (36), log_contract tests (17), verification_runner tests (26), scenario catalog validation, cross-epic script conformance, logging field audit, schema version consistency, marker format sync, full harness suite (204 tests).  {"id":"bd-huh","title":"EPIC: ext4 Write Path","description":"# EPIC: ext4 Write Path

## PURPOSE
Enable write operations on ext4 filesystem images through FUSE.

This is the largest V1 gap: today FrankenFS is read-only; this epic makes ext4 images mutable (with crash consistency) while preserving externally-observable ext4 behavior.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gaps:
- full compatibility-mode write-path equivalence

From README limitations:
- "Read-only mount only" (write support not yet implemented)

The ext4 write path requires orchestrating:
1. Transaction boundaries (MVCC for concurrency control)
2. Block allocation and freeing (ext4 mballoc semantics)
3. Extent tree mutation (insert/split/merge + overlap handling)
4. Inode updates and checksums
5. Directory entry mutation (create/unlink/rename)
6. Journal write path (JBD2-compatible logging) and replay integration
7. FUSE + CLI surfaces (RW mount flag and write ops)

## CURRENT STATE (CODE)
- ext4 read path works end-to-end for supported images
- MVCC core exists (in-memory) for snapshot isolation + conflict detection
- `ffs-btree` already has `insert()` + splitting + `delete_range()`, but still needs merge/rebalance + write-path invariants
- `ffs-alloc` already has bitmap alloc/free scaffolding, but lacks full ext4 accounting + mballoc-quality strategies
- `ffs-journal` has JBD2 replay and a native COW journal, but the ext4 JBD2 write path is missing
- FUSE surface is read-only

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Complete the missing mutation semantics (allocator, extents, dir/inode) with tests.
2. Implement journal writer + integrate commit/checkpoint semantics.
3. Expose write ops via `FsOps` and FUSE with an explicit `--rw` flag.
4. Add E2E write suite that proves persistence and basic crash behavior.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `echo "test" > /mnt/ffs/newfile.txt` works.
2. `mkdir /mnt/ffs/newdir` works.
3. `rm /mnt/ffs/file` works.
4. Changes persist after unmount/remount.
5. Crash recovery via journal replay.
6. Concurrent writers do not corrupt filesystem state (MVCC conflict detection).

## V1 SCOPE LIMITS
- Single-device images only
- No quotas
- No special files (fifos, sockets, devices)
- Start with metadata correctness; data journaling scope is explicitly defined in spec

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §5 (Write Path)
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §2 (ext4 write behavior)

## Success Criteria
1. `scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh` passes on a generated ext4 image and produces actionable logs/artifacts.
2. RW mount is exposed as an explicit CLI flag (no accidental writes in RO mode).
3. Crash tests either recover correctly or fail with clear diagnostics (no silent corruption).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:15.648865297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:04:46.373119558Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:04:46.373100072Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":116,"issue_id":"bd-huh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
